title: Pinus montana
taxonomy: Pinus montana is classified as a species of pine tree in the family Pinaceae. It belongs to the genus Pinus, which is a group of coniferous trees commonly found in mountainous regions. The species is further classified as Pinus montana, with some sources considering it a synonym of Pinus wallichiana. However, the exact classification may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research. Further studies may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of this species.
uses: Pinus montana has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region, where its extracts are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The resin and essential oils from the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The antibacterial properties of Pinus montana are also thought to help in wound healing and preventing infection. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Pinus montana for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Pinus montana is a plant that is known for its medicinal properties, and its active constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The flavonoids present in Pinus montana, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to have protective effects against oxidative stress and inflammation. intro:
There is no plant called Pinus montana. However, I can provide you with information on Pinus, the genus of pine trees, and Pinus montana may be another name for Pinus wallichiana or Pinus roxburghii.

That being said, if we assume Pinus montana refers to a different species, it is a coniferous evergreen tree.
